title: Oreogrammitis temehaniensis
taxonomy: Oreogrammitis temehaniensis is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. The genus Oreogrammitis is a group of ferns native to New Caledonia. Oreogrammitis temehaniensis is a part of the plant kingdom, specifically belonging to the division Pteridophyta. This fern species has been identified and classified by botanists to provide a clear understanding of its unique characteristics. The classification of Oreogrammitis temehaniensis reflects its evolutionary history and distinct features within the plant kingdom.

intro: Oreogrammitis temehaniensis, also known as Tahitian fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to French Polynesia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Oreogrammitis temehaniensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, this plant is used to make infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties.
